2605.19382.title: PRISM: A Benchmark for Programmatic Spatial-Temporal Reasoning
2605.19382.paper_summary: Programmatic video generation through code offers geometric precision and temporal coherence beyond pixel-level diffusion models, yet rigorously evaluating whether language models can produce spatially correct animated outputs remains an open problem. We introduce PRISM, a large-scale benchmark of 10,372 human-calibrated instruction-code pairs (20 times larger than prior programmatic video generation benchmarks), grounded in real-world knowledge visualization scenarios across English and Chinese and spanning 437 subject categories. We further propose a funnel-style evaluation framework with four complementary metrics: Code-Level Reliability for executability, Spatial Reasoning for layout correctness over full animation sequences, and Prompt-Aware Dynamic Visual Complexity (PADVC) and Temporal Density (TD) for diagnosing dynamic expression and temporal activity. Systematic evaluation of seven mainstream LLMs reveals a striking Execution-Spatial Gap: the average drop from execution success rate to spatial pass rate is approximately 41%, showing that runnable code does not necessarily yield spatially coherent visual output. These findings show that programmatic video generation evaluation should go beyond executability. PRISM provides a principled benchmark for advancing spatially coherent code generation.
2605.19382.paper_summary_zh: 程式化視頻生成透過代碼提供了超越像素級擴散模型的幾何精確性和時間一致性，但嚴格評估語言模型是否能產生空間正確的動畫輸出仍然是一個未解決的問題。我們介紹了PRISM，一個包含10,372個經人類校準的指令-代碼對的大型基準（是先前程式化視頻生成基準的20倍），基於英語和中文的現實世界知識視覺化場景，涵蓋437個主題類別。我們進一步提出了一個漏斗式評估框架，包含四個互補的指標：代碼級可靠性用於可執行性，空間推理用於整個動畫序列的佈局正確性，以及提示感知動態視覺複雜性（PADVC）和時間密度（TD）用於診斷動態表達和時間活動。對七個主流LLM的系統評估顯示出驚人的執行-空間差距：從執行成功率到空間通過率的平均下降約為41%，顯示可執行的代碼不一定產生空間一致的視覺輸出。這些發現表明，程式化視頻生成的評估應超越可執行性。PRISM提供了一個原則性的基準，以推進空間一致的代碼生成。
2605.19382.author: Qiran Zhang et.al.
2605.19382.authors: Qiran Zhang, Yuheng Wang, Runde Yang, Lin Wu, Jingru Fan, Shu Yao, Jie Zhang, Tianle Zhou, Huatao Li, Ruijie Shi, Yihan Li, Chen Qian
